title: Raul Salles de Padua's Post - LinkedIn
full_text:
𝗧𝗵𝗲 𝗔𝗜-𝗮𝗴𝗲𝗻𝘁 𝘀𝘁𝗼𝗿𝘆 𝘀𝗵𝗶𝗳𝘁𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗳𝗿𝗼𝗺 𝗵𝘆𝗽𝗲 𝗰𝘆𝗰𝗹𝗲 𝘁𝗼 𝗵𝗮𝗿𝗱 𝗻𝘂𝗺𝗯𝗲𝗿𝘀.
𝗧𝗵𝗲 𝗔𝗜-𝗮𝗴𝗲𝗻𝘁 𝘀𝘁𝗼𝗿𝘆 𝘀𝗵𝗶𝗳𝘁𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗳𝗿𝗼𝗺 𝗵𝘆𝗽𝗲 𝗰𝘆𝗰𝗹𝗲 𝘁𝗼 𝗵𝗮𝗿𝗱 𝗻𝘂𝗺𝗯𝗲𝗿𝘀. Record funding, fat median rounds, and a spike in board-room chatter all point to one theme: specialized agents are where the capital and the customers are converging. This is what CB Insights’ Q1 2025 data shows.
Q1 2025 snapshot
- $66.6 B in AI funding across 1,134 deals -> up 51 % QoQ, the largest quarter on record.
- Median AI deal size hit $5 M (a four-year high), 67 % bigger than non-AI rounds.
- Agent mindshare is exploding: earnings-call mentions of “AI agents” grew 4× QoQ in Q4 ’24, and 63 % of enterprises say agents sit on their 12-month roadmap.
- Vertical traction: healthcare produced 6 of 11 new AI unicorns, while agent platforms accounted for the 3 largest AI M&A deals of the quarter.
Why it matters for startups
- Specialize or struggle. Investors are rewarding deep workflow focus over broad copilots, translating into larger but more selective rounds.
- Bigger checks = higher scrutiny. A $5 M median means founders must show rigorous cost-to-value math and enterprise-grade observability from day one.
- Exit lanes are real. Consolidation in agent infrastructure suggests defensible modules - data curation, tool-use sandboxes, eval dashboards are all acquisition magnets.
Why it matters for enterprises & investors
- Budgets unlocked, but ROI rules. Expectation of pilots to hit a clear P&L impact within 90 days.
- Talent leverage, not replacement. Early adopters see wins when agents extend specialist teams rather than replace them - no need to freak out you're going to get replaced -> you should embrace it instead.
